answer: The purpose of the 'Hello World' tutorial on GitHub is to teach GitHub essentials like repositories, branches, commits, and pull requests, and to help you learn GitHub's pull request workflow.

You can think of a repository as a folder that contains related items, such as files, images, videos, or even other folders. A repository usually groups together items that belong to the same "project" or thing you're working on.
A README file is a file with information about your project.

Commits are saved changes to the files in your repository on GitHub. Each commit has an associated commit message, which is a description explaining why a particular change was made. Commit messages capture the history of your changes so that other contributors can understand what you’ve done and why.
After you merge your pull request, the changes on your branch will be incorporated into the main branch.
